app.wslNotFound: ⚠ WSL not found — bash tool unavailable

sidebar.unpinMessage: You dragged a pinned chat to recent. Are you sure you want to unpin it?
sidebar.pinMessage: You dragged a recent chat to pinned. Are you sure you want to pin it?

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: A project has its own prompt and files. They are automatically passed to the model in every project chat — Word/PDF/Excel files as text immediately.
projects.empty: No projects yet. Create one above.
projects.deleteMessage: Project files will be deleted. Chats will remain but lose their connection to the project.
projects.noChats: No chats yet — start a new one above.
projects.instructionsEmpty: Add a prompt — it is passed to the model in every project chat.
projects.filesEmpty: Word, PDF, Excel and text files are instantly turned into text for the model.
projects.instructionsHint: Example: "Answer concisely, in Russian. You are an assistant for Project X."
projects.fileTextEmpty: Text not extracted — the model will open the file directly if needed.

allChats.title: All chats
allChats.emptySubtitle: Try a different query or create a new chat

settings.errorHint: An error occurred communicating with the server. Please check if the backend is running, or try again.

general.title: General
general.description: Personal settings and default app behavior. Everything stored locally — no cloud accounts.
general.profileDescription: How the model addresses you. Not sent anywhere.
general.avatarHint: Shown in the sidebar. Stored only locally.
general.avatarFormats: JPG, PNG, WEBP · cropped to square
general.userNameHint: The model uses this name when addressing you. Can be left empty.
general.signOutDescription: Reset name, avatar, and optionally delete all chats.
general.appearanceDescription: Light, dark, or system theme. Full palette customizable in CSS.
general.themeHint: Color scheme of the entire interface.
general.sandboxDescription: File and shell access boundary. By default the model only sees the current chat folder.
general.unrestrictedDescription: Full PC access — model can read ~/.ssh, AppData and write anywhere. Only enable if you understand the risk.
general.sandboxRemoved: Sandbox removed. Model and agent have full access to WSL and Windows.
general.sandboxReadAnyFile: Model can read any files, including ~/.ssh and AppData.
general.sandboxNoIsolation: bash and other tools work without isolation.
general.sandboxRestartAgent: Restart the agent to restore the sandbox.
general.terminalDescription: Which shell the agent uses to execute commands. Default is bash inside WSL, with automatic fallback to Windows PowerShell if WSL is not installed.
general.shellPreferenceHint: Auto — bash in WSL, fallback to PowerShell on error. WSL only — error if WSL unavailable. PowerShell only — never call WSL.
general.switchToPowershellHint: WSL unavailable — switch to Windows PowerShell
general.powershellWarning: PowerShell mode. bwrap sandbox is unavailable on Windows — the model is limited to the chat folder via path checks, no kernel-level isolation.

signOut.title: Sign out
signOut.subtitle: Choose what to do with your data
signOut.deleteAllHint: Chats, history and personal data will be erased
signOut.keepChatsHint: Only name and avatar will be reset

about.title: About
about.description: AgentChat — desktop chat for work tasks and brainstorming. Local, private, no code editor bindings.
about.goalText: A chat to discuss work questions, brainstorm ideas, analyze problems — without diving into a development environment. No hidden script execution, no terminal magic. Just a conversation.

mcp.title: MCP Servers
mcp.description: External Model Context Protocol servers. Enable them next to the model in chat.
mcp.empty: No servers. Add one via the form or import from claude_desktop_config.json.
mcp.name: Name
mcp.importDescription: Paste claude_desktop_config.json contents or an { mcpServers: ... } object. Existing servers with the same id will be overwritten.

models.title: Models
models.description: Default model and generation parameters. The full model list is managed in the Providers tab.
models.defaultModelHint: Which model to use for new chats. Models are grouped by provider. Switch anytime in the chat header.
models.noModels: — no models loaded. Add a provider key and refresh. —
models.temperatureHint: Controls randomness. Lower = more focused, higher = more creative. 0.7 is a good default for most tasks.
models.generationParamsHint: Temperature and iteration limits applied to all new chats by default.
models.maxIterationsHint: from {{min}} to {{max}}. How many tool-use steps the agent can take before stopping.

providers.title: Providers
providers.description: Models loaded from {api_base}/models of each provider.
providers.validationRequired: ID, name and api_base are required
providers.validationFormat: ID: only latin letters, numbers, _ and -
providers.name: Name
providers.modelsEmptyHint: No models. Add a key and refresh.

paths.title: Paths
paths.description: Storage locations and backend connection settings. All paths are relative to the app data directory.
paths.storageHint: Where skills and working files are stored on disk. These are managed automatically.
paths.connectionHint: Address of the backend server. Leave empty for local mode (default). Set a remote address to connect from another device.
paths.skillsFolderHint: Extracted .skill packages. Watchdog monitors for changes.
paths.backendUrlHint: Leave empty for local (default). Set a server address for remote access from your phone.
paths.remoteAccessHint: Expose this backend so a phone can connect. Protected by a token — only devices that scan the code get in.
paths.remoteAllowHint: Binds the backend to your network and requires a token. Restart the backend to apply.
paths.remoteScan: Scan with your phone's camera to connect:
paths.remoteRestart: Restart the backend for the change to take effect.
paths.remoteLocalOnly: Remote access is managed on the host computer, not from a connected device.
paths.remoteNoUrls: No network address detected. Use your PC's LAN IP or a Tailscale address.
paths.remoteTailscaleHint: Same Wi‑Fi: use the address above. From anywhere: install Tailscale on both devices and use its address — `tailscale serve` also gives HTTPS, which makes the app installable to the home screen.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Extend model capabilities. The manifest is rebuilt automatically when the skills folder changes.
skills.installDescription: Paste a GitHub repo URL, .skill archive, or any URL serving SKILL.md.
skills.presetDescription: Word, Excel, PowerPoint, PDF, skill creation — official collection from github.com/anthropics/skills.
skills.installedDescription: Click a row to expand SKILL.md. Menu on the right to delete.

onboarding.validationProvider: Select a provider and enter an API key
onboarding.wslStarting: Starting WSL + Ubuntu installation (UAC may be required)…
onboarding.wslWaiting: Waiting for distro to be ready (first boot may take up to 10 min)…
onboarding.wslTimeout: WSL did not start within 10 minutes — try manually via PowerShell
onboarding.wslInstallingDeps: Installing Node, Python, pandoc, LibreOffice, poppler-utils, docx…
onboarding.step1Description: Your name will be passed to the model in the system prompt.
onboarding.step2Title: Connect a provider and choose a model
onboarding.step2Description: Enter an API key for at least one provider. The model list will update automatically.
onboarding.step2NoModels: List is empty. Save a key and click Refresh.
onboarding.step3Description: The agent's bash tool runs inside WSL. One click installs Ubuntu and all libraries for office formats: Node.js, Python 3, pandoc, LibreOffice, poppler-utils, and the npm docx package.
onboarding.step4Description: A ready-made toolkit that extends the model: document creation and office format handling.
onboarding.skillCreate: Skill creation — meta-skill for writing your own

artifacts.downloadHint: click Download to open in native app
artifacts.panelClosed: Preview closed — click Open on a file
artifacts.noFiles: Files created by the agent will appear here

morning: Good morning, {name}! Where shall we start?
Good morning! Coffee is still hot — perfect timing.
Good morning, {name}. The world awaits your ideas.
Morning, {name}! Coffee first — everything else after.
A good morning for good decisions, {name}.
Rise and shine, {name}! Deadlines are already up.
Good morning! Today is a good day to break something and fix it.
Morning. Plan for the day: don't lose your mind.
First cup of coffee down. Loading consciousness...
day: Good afternoon! What shall we do?
Midday is a good moment to do something important, {name}.
Good afternoon, {name}. What's on the agenda?
Good afternoon! Tasks won't solve themselves.
Snack time is the best time for refactoring.
Equator of the day passed. Half the bugs are still ahead.
Good afternoon! Breathe in, breathe out — and keep going.
evening: Good evening, {name}! How was your day?
Good evening! A little more and you can rest.
Good evening! The evening sprint is the most honest one.
Thoughts are clearer in the evening, {name}. Write.
Good evening. One last push — I'll help.
Sun is down, monitors are shining brighter.
Good evening! Hope dinner tasted better than your code.
Darkness falls. Time for vampires and productive programmers.
night: Everyone's asleep and you're here. Must be important.
{name}, it's already tomorrow. Or still yesterday.
Quiet hour for the boldest ideas.
Dark interface theme and a dark night outside.
You should go to bed, {name}. But since we're here...
Bugs love silence. Let's catch them.
any: Another sprint, another tech debt, {name}.
Git blame points at you, but we won't tell anyone.
sudo make me a sandwich, {name}.
Stack Overflow down? I'm here.
If it works, don't touch it. If it doesn't, write here.
Deadline tomorrow? Sounds like 'starting today'.
Bug or feature — decide for yourself, {name}.
99 problems, and they're all in the console.
Ctrl+Z can undo anything except this conversation.
Coffee brewed? Then let's start.
Your IDE didn't crash today? Good sign.
Scope creeps, but we hold on.
Everything broke after the refactor? Let's roll back, {name}.
Regex is for the chosen ones. Or the insane.
Production is down, but we're calm.
How about rewriting everything in Rust?
Docs are for the weak, source code is for the strong.
Cache invalidated, neural networks warmed up.
How can I help today, {name}?
What are we planning — deeds or dreams?
The task list won't write itself.
Tell me what matters right now.
Ready to help — just write.
Soup is boiling, shall we talk?
A thousand questions in your head? Start with one.
Everything can be solved — let's figure it out.
Today's day hasn't been written yet, {name}.
Borscht on the stove, kids asleep — perfect time for AI.
Iron unplugged? Great, let's go.
While the washing machine spins, we can conquer the world, {name}.
Groceries, cooking, cleaning... or shall we write some code?
Coffee stains on the desk — a sign of genius.
Let the vacuum work while we get down to business.
Chaos is a workflow.
Bad ideas have a place too. Especially here.
Brain loading… write something.
Start typing before motivation evaporates.
You think — I help you think further.
Welcome back to productive chaos, {name}.
Procrastination with extra features.
Small steps. Huge ambitions.
Another 'quick question', right, {name}?
The number of open tabs stays between us.
No pressure. Just your entire future.
Let's pretend we're confident together.
Write first — panic later.
Your ideas haven't been here yet. Show me.
Genius takes time. Or a couple of prompts.
The secret to success is acting like it was planned.
Mistakes aren't scary. Not backing up is scary.
Another sprint, another victory — almost.
Wrote some code today? The real question is: did you commit?
The build is failing, but hey — at least we tried.
Who needs a QA team when you have users?
Every 'quick fix' is a new feature in disguise.
I'd tell you a joke about recursion, but you'd need to understand it first.
Spaghetti code again? Let's untangle this together.
Your code reviewed itself out of fear.
Let's turn those bugs into features one by one.
Roadmap? We are the roadmap.
YOLO — You Only Live Once. But your code runs many times.
If at first you don't succeed, call it version 1.0.
